title: Mattress manufacturing process and apparatus
abstract: A process and apparatus for manufacturing a mattress generally includes an automated foam layer placement apparatus for accurately securing one or more foam layers onto an innercore unit and bucket assembly.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A process for accurately securing one or more foam layers onto an innercore unit and bucket assembly, the apparatus comprising: automatically sizing the foam layer by compressing and stretching the foam layer to define a nominal size; automatically applying an adhesive to a top surface of an innercore unit and bucket assembly; automatically aligning the inner core unit and bucket assembly to a base corner datum; and automatically lifting and placing the nominally sized foam layer onto the top surface of the innercore unit and bucket assembly using the base corner datum as a reference point. 2. The process of claim 1, further comprising compressing the foam layer onto the innercore unit and bucket assembly. 3. The process of claim 1, wherein the innercore unit comprises spring coils, foam, and combinations thereof. 4. The process of claim 1, wherein automatically applying an adhesive to a top surface of the innercore unit and bucket assembly comprises detecting a leading edge of the innercore unit and bucket assembly and applying a controlled amount of the adhesive in a particular pattern onto the top surface and discontinuing the adhesive upon detection of a trailing edge of the innercore unit and bucket assembly. 5. The process of claim 1, wherein automatically aligning the inner core unit and bucket assembly to a base corner datum comprises pushing the inner core unit and bucket assembly into a corner defined by against reference rails at right angles to each other. 6. The process of claim 1, wherein automatically lifting and placing the foam layer onto the top surface of the innercore unit and bucket assembly using the base corner datum as a reference point comprises attaching the foam layer to a lifting assembly via a needle gripper, wherein the needle gripper comprises retractable angled needles configured to engage the foam layer when extended. 7. The process of claim 1, wherein compressing and stretching of the foam layer comprises disposing an adjustable rail assembly about the foam layer configured to compress and stretch the foam layer to a nominal size. 8. The process of claim 7, wherein the adjustable rail assembly comprises: a pair of longitudinal rails and a pair of transverse rails to frame a periphery of the foam layer, each rail having a contact surface perpendicular to the planar surface to provide compression of the foam layer when in use; and one or more adjustable clamps disposed on each rail having a fixed portion carried by the rail and configured to slide underneath the foam layer upon compression and an adjustable portion configured to clamp down on the foam layer to permit stretching of the foam layer to a nominal size when in use. 9. The process of claim 8, wherein compressing the foam layer comprises simultaneously moving a selected one or both of the longitudinal rails towards each other and moving a selected one or both of the transverse rails towards each other. 10. The process of claim 8, wherein stretching the foam layer comprises gripping an edge of the foam layer with each one of the longitudinal and transverse rails; and simultaneously moving a selected one or both of the longitudinal rails away from each other and moving a selected one or both of the transverse rails away from each other. 11. An automated system for placing and securing one or more foam layers onto an innercore unit and bucket assembly, the system comprising: an adhesive application station for receiving an innercore unit and bucket assembly, the adhesive application station comprising a movable surface to support the innercore unit and bucket assembly and a bridge spaced from and spanning the movable surface, the bridge comprising one or more adhesive applicators oriented to discharge a controlled amount of adhesive in a defined pattern onto the a surface of the innercore unit and bucket assembly; an innercore unit and bucket assembly alignment station comprising a movable surface and an adjustable frame assembly configured to align the innercore unit and bucket assembly to a known datum point on the movable surface; a foam pick and placement station comprising a sizing table configured to compress and stretch the one or more foam layers to a nominal size; a lifting assembly comprising a plurality of lifting units spaced about an adjustable frame assembly so as to provide lifting and release of the foam layer from the sizing table when in use, and a robotic arm coupled to the adjustable frame assembly and configured to move the adjustable frame assembly from the sizing table to the innercore unit and bucket assembly alignment station; and a compression station comprising a vertically adjustable platen spaced above a movable surface. 12. The automated system of claim 11, wherein the movable surfaces of the adhesive application station, the innercore unit and bucket assembly alignment station, and the compression station are serially aligned and coplanar to one another to receive and discharge the innercore unit and bucket assembly during processing, and wherein the foam pick and placement station is immediately adjacent to the innercore unit and bucket assembly alignment station. 13. The automated system of claim 11, further comprising a manufacturing execution system and programmable logic control for planning, scheduling, and controlling the adhesive application station, the innercore unit and bucket assembly alignment station, the lifting assembly, and the compression station. 14. The automated system of claim 11, wherein each one of the plurality of lifting units comprises a housing supporting two slides, each slide provided with one or more of the retractable angled needles, wherein the one or more retractable angled needles on one slide face toward the one or more retractable angled needles of the other slide. 15. The automated system of claim 14, wherein the angle of the one or more retractable needles in one slide is in a range of about 30 degrees to about 60 degrees relative to the plane of the foam layer and the angle of the one or more retractable needles in the other slide is in a range of about 120 to about 150 degrees relative to the plane of the foam layer such that the one or more retractable needles in the one slide and the other slide converge upon extension from the respective sliding. 16. The automated system of claim 14, wherein the angle of the one or more retractable needles in one slide is about 45 degrees relative to the plane of the foam layer and the angle of the one or more retractable needles in the other slide is about 135 degrees relative to the plane of the foam layer such that the one or more retractable needles in the one slide and the other slide converge upon extension from the respective sliding. 17. The automated system of claim 11, wherein the adhesive application station further comprises a motion sensor for detecting a leading and trailing edge of the innercore unit and bucket assembly. 18. The automated system of claim 11, wherein the sizing table comprises an adjustable rail assembly comprising a pair of longitudinal rails and a pair of transverse rails to frame a periphery of the foam layer, each rail having a contact surface perpendicular to the planar surface to provide compression of the foam layer when in use; and one or more adjustable clamps disposed on each rail having a fixed portion carried by the rail and configured to slide underneath the foam layer upon compression and an adjustable portion 

 configured to clamp down on the foam layer to permit stretching of the foam layer to a nominal size when in use. 19. The automated system of claim 11, wherein the sizing table further comprises a plurality of perforations in fluid communication with a vacuum.
